user_query:
I'm coordinating a small volunteer team to prepare a community workshop on "Moldovan identity after the Soviet period." Using the themes and materials in the input folder, please produce a synchronization pack for our next planning meeting that combines credible web research with updated local documents and clear assignments.

Required inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/themes.csv — authoritative list of themes (T1–T6)
- input/team_roster.csv — volunteers and roles for assignment
- input/exhibit_outline.md — current outline with TODOs and missing citations

Your tasks and required outputs:
1) Internet research and structured bibliography
- For each theme in input/themes.csv, perform at least one search engine query that combines the theme title with Moldova-related terms. Use the queries to identify credible, citable resources relevant to that theme.
- Collect a total of 7–10 resources across all themes with these constraints:
  - Include at least one resource from an official Moldovan institution (e.g., domains operated by Moldovan government or national cultural bodies) and at least one from a recognized international organization (e.g., intergovernmental or cultural bodies, not personal blogs).
  - Avoid personal blogs and unsourced opinion pieces; prefer institutional reports, museum/archive pages, laws/acts, or publications from reputable organizations.
- Save the results to outputs/sources.json as a list of objects with the following fields for each item:
  - id: unique string like "SRC1", "SRC2", ...
  - title: resource title as shown on the page or document
  - organization: hosting or issuing organization
  - host_domain: domain of the site (e.g., example.gov.md, example.org)
  - resource_type: one of [report|law/act|archive page|museum page|article|other]
  - year: 4-digit year if available; otherwise leave blank
  - access_date: the date you accessed it
  - query_used: the exact search query string you used to find it
  - theme_ids: list of theme IDs from input/themes.csv that this resource supports (e.g., ["T2","T3"]) 
  - summary: one or two sentences explaining why it’s relevant to those themes

2) Theme-to-source mapping
- Create outputs/theme_source_map.csv with columns: theme_id,source_ids
  - Each theme_id from input/themes.csv must appear exactly once.
  - source_ids is a comma-separated list of resource IDs from outputs/sources.json that support that theme (at least one per theme).
  - Only use IDs that actually exist in outputs/sources.json.

3) Update the exhibit outline
- Edit input/exhibit_outline.md to resolve TODOs and add citations, then save the updated file as outputs/exhibit_outline_updated.md.
  - Preserve the original structure and headings.
  - For each bullet that references a T# theme, remove the "[needs source]" marker and add in-text citation tags immediately at the end in the form [SRC:ID] or [SRC:ID1, SRC:ID2], using IDs from outputs/sources.json according to your mapping.
  - Remove remaining TODO placeholders while keeping the intent (e.g., slightly tighten phrasing of the purpose line without changing meaning).
  - Ensure every theme T1–T6 has at least one [SRC:...] tag in the updated outline.

4) Prepare meeting notes with assignments
- Create outputs/meeting_notes.md with these sections:
  - Agenda: 3–5 bullets focused on reviewing sources and finalizing which to feature for each theme.
  - Research Updates: brief summary of how many sources were found per theme.
  - Decisions Needed: bullets listing any themes that currently have only one source or where sources need verification.
  - Action Items: a table or list where each theme_id is assigned to one volunteer from input/team_roster.csv, chosen by best match of the volunteer’s focus_area to the theme; include volunteer name, email, theme_id, the chosen source_ids for that theme, and set due:TBD.
- Ensure every theme has exactly one responsible volunteer in this section.

General notes and constraints:
- Do not include direct URLs in this instruction; you may record URLs you find inside outputs/sources.json if helpful, but it’s optional. The important part is that the fields listed above are present and consistent.
- The sources you choose must align with the themes and be plausibly citable by a community group (institutional pages, reports, laws, or museum/archive materials are ideal).
- The mapping in outputs/theme_source_map.csv must be consistent with both outputs/sources.json and outputs/exhibit_outline_updated.md (i.e., every theme in the map appears in the updated outline with matching [SRC:...] IDs, and all IDs used exist in outputs/sources.json).
- Deliverables to create: outputs/sources.json, outputs/theme_source_map.csv, outputs/exhibit_outline_updated.md, outputs/meeting_notes.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
